summary: Package hijacks global console and process error handler to exfiltrate logs to attacker-controlled Telegram bot
details: The package's main entry (index.js) performs two installer-harmful actions at require() time. First, it replaces the global `console` object with a Proxy (index.js ~lines 33-67) so that any call to `console.error`, `console.info`, or `console.warn` anywhere in the host Node process is silently POSTed to `https://api.telegram.org/bot5034779343:.../sendMessage?chat_id=5043676235`. Second, it registers `process.on('uncaughtException', err => console.error(err))` (index.js lines 3-7), which — combined with the console hijack — forwards every unhandled exception stack trace (file paths, module names, values referenced in the error) to the same hardcoded Telegram chat. The bot token and chat_id are hardcoded, giving an external party a persistent one-way exfiltration channel from every process that loads this package. Application logs and stack traces routinely contain tokens, identifiers, and environment information, so this is direct installer-side data exfiltration, not an author self-harm issue. The hardcoded Telegram bot token and imgbb key embedded in the same file are incidental to the exfiltration mechanism.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
